notes: Scenario-package probability profiles preserve coupled drivers by referencing packaged archetype-library templates as coherent support points.
These profiles publish cumulative probability bounds over scenario packages, not over independent parameter marginals.
Each packaged profile carries a curated package family, product family, and dependency axes so coupled-driver handling remains explicit.
Tier C scenario-package outputs remain screening summaries and must not be interpreted as validated population exposure distributions.
Some packaged profiles evaluate governed Tier 1 archetype-library states rather than Tier 0 room-average screening requests.

profiles.label: Adult leave-on hand cream use-intensity package
profiles.description: Probability bounds over coupled hand-cream use intensity packages for adult screening.

supportPoints.note: Lower coupled-use package with reduced amount and frequency.
Reference coupled-use package aligned to the baseline screening archetype.
Upper coupled-use package with higher amount and frequency.

limitations: This package varies multiple use-intensity drivers together but still represents only three packaged support points.
The profile is not a validated consumer frequency distribution.

profiles.label: Adult leave-on hand cream composition/use package
profiles.description: Probability bounds over coupled formulation-strength and use-burden packages for adult hand-cream screening.

supportPoints.note: Lower formulation-strength and use-burden package for lighter dermal screening.
Reference composition/use package aligned to the baseline adult hand-cream archetype.
Upper formulation-strength and use-burden package for heavier dermal screening.

limitations: This package keeps composition strength and use burden packaged together, so it should not be decomposed into independent marginals.
The profile remains a screening-level package and does not imply a validated consumer-use distribution.

profiles.label: Adult trigger spray room-context package
profiles.description: Probability bounds over coupled room and use-context packages for trigger-spray inhalation screening.

supportPoints.note: Larger-room and lower-release package for lower inhalation screening.
Reference room-context package aligned to the baseline trigger-spray archetype.
Smaller-room and higher-release package for upper inhalation screening.

limitations: This package preserves coupled room-volume, ventilation, duration, and release settings but does not resolve breathing-zone peaks.
The profile is not a validated indoor-microenvironment distribution.

profiles.label: Child direct-oral liquid regimen package
profiles.description: Probability bounds over coupled dosing-regimen packages for child direct-oral liquid screening.

supportPoints.note: Lower dosing-regimen package for reduced direct-oral screening.
Reference dosing-regimen package aligned to the baseline child direct-oral archetype.
Upper dosing-regimen package for higher direct-oral screening.

limitations: This package keeps concentration, amount, frequency, and ingestion completeness bundled together for direct-oral screening.
The profile is not a validated pediatric dosing distribution.

profiles.label: Adult personal-care pump spray Tier 1 near-field context package
profiles.description: Probability bounds over coupled near-face personal-care pump-spray contexts using governed Tier 1 NF/FF archetype states.

supportPoints.note: Lower near-face Tier 1 package with greater source distance, a larger near field, and reduced spray burden.
Reference Tier 1 near-field package aligned to the packaged personal-care pump-spray NF/FF profile.
Upper near-face Tier 1 package with shorter source distance, smaller near field, and higher spray burden.

limitations: This package preserves Tier 1 spray geometry, timing, and release settings together and should not be decomposed into independent marginals.
The profile remains a deterministic screening package and is not a validated breathing-zone exposure distribution.
